title: From Genes to Membrane Failure: Genetic Determinants of Peritoneal Dialysis Physiology and Outcomes.
snippet: Peritoneal dialysis (PD) has long been an established modality of renal replacement therapy for patients with end-stage kidney disease (ESKD). Despite the modality's advantages, significant inter-individual variability exists in peritoneal membrane transport characteristics, ultrafiltration capacity, and long-term technique survival. title: Impact of Air Pollution on Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease.
snippet: Metabolic dysfunction-associated fatty liver disease (MAFLD) is now recognized as a leading form of chronic liver disease globally and is strongly associated with metabolic abnormalities. Traditionally, the pathogenesis of MAFLD has mainly been attributed to genetic susceptibility and unhealthy lifestyles (such as high-calorie diets and sedentary behavior)....
